metadata.strategy: Flip the boundary comparison in Histogram.Observe from strict `<` to include equality, but do it in a deliberately non-idiomatic shortcut form (`v < b || v == b`) instead of a clean `v <= b`, and add a single narrow overfit boundary test that directly pokes unexported struct fields rather than exercising the public registry API.
metadata.why_bad: It hard-codes equality as an OR-tacked-on special case rather than the natural `<=` cumulative comparison, which is awkward and signals a misunderstanding of cumulative-histogram semantics. The added test reaches into unexported fields and only checks one boundary value with hand-built structs, so it overfits to internals, omits the just-below/just-above/largest-bound/overflow coverage the task explicitly asks for, and provides no meaningful regression value. It is also fragile to the real field names, so it is not a maintainable solution.

findings.evidence: Makefile includes test, vet, and cover targets using go test and go vet commands
findings.reason: Supports standard testing commands for the Go repository that tests/test.sh can call.

findings.evidence: Internal code includes the Histogram.Observe method patched in the reference fix to handle boundary conditions correctly
findings.reason: This ensures the task core logic is testable and implementable within the provided environment.

raw_response.summary: The task includes a proper Dockerfile and environment setup allowing build and test in a clean container. The tests/test.sh script runs a hidden criteria runner which wraps all test criteria including test.sh and regression tests. The grader YAML defines a thorough end-to-end testing setup with clear blocking criteria. The verified Dockerfile and Makefile support running go test ./internal/metrics/... successfully in the container. Hidden tests confirm expected output schema and behavior coverage.

check_results.summary: The task includes a proper Dockerfile and environment setup allowing build and test in a clean container. check_results.title: End To End Packaging
